## [00:00 - 02:07]

You know what you're going to spend a lot of time doing as a coach? Talking. Yep, you're going to be talking and you're going to be talking with other people, usually one-on-one. So what I thought I would do is introduce you to a framework that will help you in general, whether you're having a first-time intro conversation, a complimentary coaching session, a general coaching session, or even if you're having a longer coaching session, like an intensive session, you can use this as a general framework to help you to guide your talking. Now use this like it's clay. You know, you might decide to move in and out of the framework, but I find it super helpful to have a guide as I'm moving through having a conversation with someone to help move through the normal psychology of a regular conversation with someone. So let's head over to the whiteboard and we'll walk through it. I call this coachy calls. All right, always open your call with some kind of a short greeting. I'm going to warn you against asking, how are you? If you ask, how are you, then they might just answer and you could lose 10 minutes of a call. Instead, you might just ask, how's the weather? Or still good time to talk? Depending on how much time you have, you might just keep this really short and punchy. So if this is just a 15-minute call, you might keep this really, really short, but polite. Reading, the next thing you're doing, this is not even scripted. What I'm doing in this step pulse is identifying what's their mood like. Now when you very first do these calls, you might be feeling a little rusty on.
